## Logging Infrastructure
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/lib/logging.sh

LOG_LEVEL=${CAUT_TEST_LOG_LEVEL:-INFO}
LOG_FILE="${CAUT_TEST_LOG_DIR:-/tmp}/caut_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
LOG_TO_STDERR=${CAUT_TEST_LOG_STDERR:-1}

# ANSI colors for terminal output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
GRAY='\033[0;90m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

log_debug() { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" =~ DEBUG|TRACE ]] && _log "DEBUG" "$GRAY" "$@"; }
log_info()  { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" =~ INFO|DEBUG|TRACE ]] && _log "INFO" "$CYAN" "$@"; }
log_warn()  { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" =~ WARN|INFO|DEBUG|TRACE ]] && _log "WARN" "$YELLOW" "$@"; }
log_error() { _log "ERROR" "$RED" "$@"; }
log_pass()  { _log "PASS" "$GREEN" "$@"; }
log_fail()  { _log "FAIL" "$RED" "$@"; }

_log() {
    local level=$1
    local color=$2
    shift 2
    local timestamp=$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N)
    local caller="${FUNCNAME[2]:-main}:${BASH_LINENO[1]:-0}"
    
    # Plain text for log file
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] [$caller] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Colored for stderr if enabled
    if [[ "$LOG_TO_STDERR" == "1" ]]; then
        echo -e "${GRAY}[$timestamp]${NC} ${color}[$level]${NC} ${GRAY}[$caller]${NC} $*" >&2
    fi
}

log_command() {
    local cmd="$*"
    log_debug "Executing: $cmd"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%N)
    local output
    output=$("$@" 2>&1)
    local exit_code=$?
    local end_time=$(date +%s%N)
    local duration_ms=$(( (end_time - start_time) / 1000000 ))
    log_debug "Exit code: $exit_code, Duration: ${duration_ms}ms"
    log_debug "Output length: ${#output} chars"
    if [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" == "TRACE" ]]; then
        log_debug "Full output:\n$output"
    else
        log_debug "Output (first 500 chars): ${output:0:500}"
    fi
    echo "$output"
    return $exit_code
}

log_section() {
    local title="$1"
    log_info "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    log_info "  $title"
    log_info "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
}

log_test_start() {
    log_info "▶ TEST: $1"
}

log_test_end() {
    local name="$1"
    local passed=$2
    if [[ $passed -eq 1 ]]; then
        log_pass "✓ $name"
    else
        log_fail "✗ $name"
    fi
}
```

## Test Assertions
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/lib/assertions.sh

assert_contains_ansi() {
    local output="$1"
    local description="$2"
    # Check for ANSI escape sequences (ESC[)
    if echo -e "$output" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
        log_pass "$description - contains ANSI codes"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - expected ANSI codes, got plain text"
        log_debug "Output sample: ${output:0:200}"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_no_ansi() {
    local output="$1"
    local description="$2"
    if ! echo -e "$output" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
        log_pass "$description - no ANSI codes"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - unexpected ANSI codes found"
        # Show the ANSI codes found
        log_debug "ANSI codes found: $(echo -e "$output" | grep -oP '\x1b\[[0-9;]*m' | head -5 | tr '\n' ' ')"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_valid_json() {
    local output="$1"
    local description="$2"
    local jq_error
    if jq_error=$(echo "$output" | jq . 2>&1 >/dev/null); then
        log_pass "$description - valid JSON"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - invalid JSON"
        log_debug "JSON parse error: $jq_error"
        log_debug "First 300 chars: ${output:0:300}"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_exit_code() {
    local expected=$1
    local actual=$2
    local description="$3"
    if [[ $actual -eq $expected ]]; then
        log_pass "$description - exit code $actual"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - expected exit $expected, got $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_contains() {
    local output="$1"
    local expected="$2"
    local description="$3"
    if [[ "$output" == *"$expected"* ]]; then
        log_pass "$description - contains '$expected'"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - missing '$expected'"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_not_contains() {
    local output="$1"
    local unexpected="$2"
    local description="$3"
    if [[ "$output" != *"$unexpected"* ]]; then
        log_pass "$description - does not contain '$unexpected'"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - unexpectedly contains '$unexpected'"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_matches_regex() {
    local output="$1"
    local pattern="$2"
    local description="$3"
    if echo "$output" | grep -qP "$pattern"; then
        log_pass "$description - matches pattern"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - does not match pattern '$pattern'"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_line_count() {
    local output="$1"
    local min_lines=$2
    local max_lines=$3
    local description="$4"
    local actual_lines=$(echo "$output" | wc -l)
    if [[ $actual_lines -ge $min_lines && $actual_lines -le $max_lines ]]; then
        log_pass "$description - $actual_lines lines (expected $min_lines-$max_lines)"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "$description - $actual_lines lines (expected $min_lines-$max_lines)"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Safety Gate Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/safety_gates/test_env_vars.sh

set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/logging.sh"
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/assertions.sh"

CAUT="${CAUT_BINARY:-target/release/caut}"
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

test_no_color_env() {
    log_test_start "NO_COLOR environment variable disables colors"
    
    local output
    output=$(NO_COLOR=1 script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "NO_COLOR=1"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_caut_plain_env() {
    log_test_start "CAUT_PLAIN environment variable disables rich output"
    
    local output
    output=$(CAUT_PLAIN=1 script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "CAUT_PLAIN=1"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_ci_env() {
    log_test_start "CI environment variable disables rich output"
    
    local output
    output=$(CI=true script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "CI=true"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_github_actions_env() {
    log_test_start "GITHUB_ACTIONS environment variable disables rich output"
    
    local output
    output=$(GITHUB_ACTIONS=true script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "GITHUB_ACTIONS=true"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_term_dumb() {
    log_test_start "TERM=dumb disables rich output"
    
    local output
    output=$(TERM=dumb script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "TERM=dumb"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_env_precedence() {
    log_test_start "Environment variables take precedence over TTY"
    
    # Even with TTY simulation, NO_COLOR should win
    local output
    output=$(NO_COLOR=1 COLORTERM=truecolor script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "NO_COLOR overrides COLORTERM"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

# Run all tests
log_section "Safety Gate: Environment Variables"
test_no_color_env
test_caut_plain_env
test_ci_env
test_github_actions_env
test_term_dumb
test_env_precedence

log_info "Environment variable tests: $TESTS_PASSED passed, $TESTS_FAILED failed"
exit $TESTS_FAILED
```

## Pipe Detection Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/safety_gates/test_pipe_detection.sh

set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/logging.sh"
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/assertions.sh"

CAUT="${CAUT_BINARY:-target/release/caut}"
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

test_pipe_disables_rich() {
    log_test_start "Piping output disables rich formatting"
    
    # When piped, stdout is not a TTY
    local output
    output=$($CAUT usage | cat)
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "piped output"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_redirect_disables_rich() {
    log_test_start "Redirecting output disables rich formatting"
    
    local tmpfile=$(mktemp)
    $CAUT usage > "$tmpfile" 2>&1 || true
    local output=$(cat "$tmpfile")
    rm -f "$tmpfile"
    
    if assert_no_ansi "$output" "redirected output"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

test_tty_enables_rich() {
    log_test_start "TTY output enables rich formatting"
    
    # Use script to simulate TTY
    local output
    output=$(script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_contains_ansi "$output" "TTY output"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

log_section "Safety Gate: Pipe/TTY Detection"
test_pipe_disables_rich
test_redirect_disables_rich
test_tty_enables_rich

log_info "Pipe detection tests: $TESTS_PASSED passed, $TESTS_FAILED failed"
exit $TESTS_FAILED
```

## Color Depth Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/color_depth/test_truecolor.sh

set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/logging.sh"
source "$(dirname "$0")/../lib/assertions.sh"

CAUT="${CAUT_BINARY:-target/release/caut}"
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

test_truecolor_detection() {
    log_test_start "COLORTERM=truecolor enables 24-bit colors"
    
    local output
    output=$(COLORTERM=truecolor script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    # Check for RGB escape sequences (38;2;r;g;b format)
    if echo -e "$output" | grep -qP '\x1b\[38;2;'; then
        log_pass "Truecolor RGB sequences present"
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        # May fall back to 256-color, which is also acceptable
        log_info "No truecolor sequences found (may use 256-color fallback)"
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    fi
}

test_256color_detection() {
    log_test_start "TERM=xterm-256color enables 256 colors"
    
    local output
    output=$(TERM=xterm-256color script -q /dev/null -c "$CAUT usage" 2>&1 || true)
    
    if assert_contains_ansi "$output" "256-color mode"; then
        ((TESTS_PASSED++))
    else
        ((TESTS_FAILED++))
    fi
}

log_section "Color Depth Detection"
test_truecolor_detection
test_256color_detection

log_info "Color depth tests: $TESTS_PASSED passed, $TESTS_FAILED failed"
exit $TESTS_FAILED
```

## Master Test Runner
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/run_all_tests.sh

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/logging.sh"

log_section "CAUT E2E Test Suite"
log_info "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
log_info "Log level: $LOG_LEVEL"
log_info "Started at: $(date)"

# Build release binary
log_info "Building release binary..."
if cargo build --release 2>&1 | while read line; do log_debug "cargo: $line"; done; then
    log_pass "Build successful"
else
    log_fail "Build failed"
    exit 1
fi
export CAUT_BINARY="$SCRIPT_DIR/../../target/release/caut"

# Verify binary exists
if [[ ! -x "$CAUT_BINARY" ]]; then
    log_error "Binary not found at $CAUT_BINARY"
    exit 1
fi
log_info "Using binary: $CAUT_BINARY"
log_info "Binary version: $($CAUT_BINARY --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown')"

# Discover and run all test scripts
TOTAL_PASSED=0
TOTAL_FAILED=0
TOTAL_SKIPPED=0
FAILED_TESTS=()

run_test_category() {
    local category=$1
    local category_dir="$SCRIPT_DIR/$category"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$category_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "Category directory not found: $category_dir"
        return
    fi
    
    log_section "Category: $category"
    
    for test_script in "$category_dir"/test_*.sh; do
        if [[ -f "$test_script" ]]; then
            local test_name=$(basename "$test_script" .sh)
            log_info "Running: $test_name"
            
            local start_time=$(date +%s)
            if bash "$test_script"; then
                local end_time=$(date +%s)
                local duration=$((end_time - start_time))
                log_pass "$test_name completed in ${duration}s"
                ((TOTAL_PASSED++))
            else
                local exit_code=$?
                log_fail "$test_name failed with exit code $exit_code"
                ((TOTAL_FAILED++))
                FAILED_TESTS+=("$test_name")
            fi
        fi
    done
}

# Run all test categories
run_test_category "rich_output"
run_test_category "robot_mode"
run_test_category "safety_gates"
run_test_category "themes"
run_test_category "color_depth"

# Generate JUnit XML for CI
generate_junit_xml() {
    local xml_file="$SCRIPT_DIR/results.xml"
    cat > "$xml_file" <<EOF
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<testsuites name="CAUT E2E Tests" tests="$((TOTAL_PASSED + TOTAL_FAILED))" failures="$TOTAL_FAILED" time="0">
  <testsuite name="e2e" tests="$((TOTAL_PASSED + TOTAL_FAILED))" failures="$TOTAL_FAILED">
EOF
    for test in "${FAILED_TESTS[@]:-}"; do
        echo "    <testcase name=\"$test\"><failure>Test failed</failure></testcase>" >> "$xml_file"
    done
    echo "  </testsuite>" >> "$xml_file"
    echo "</testsuites>" >> "$xml_file"
    log_info "JUnit XML written to: $xml_file"
}

generate_junit_xml

# Final report
log_section "E2E Test Summary"
log_info "Total Passed:  $TOTAL_PASSED"
log_info "Total Failed:  $TOTAL_FAILED"
log_info "Total Skipped: $TOTAL_SKIPPED"

if [[ ${#FAILED_TESTS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    log_error "Failed tests:"
    for test in "${FAILED_TESTS[@]}"; do
        log_error "  - $test"
    done
fi

log_info "Full log: $LOG_FILE"
log_info "Completed at: $(date)"

exit $TOTAL_FAILED
```

- API response headers (X-RateLimit-*, etc.)

- Local quota cache files
- gcloud CLI commands

- API keys vs. OAuth tokens
- Token refresh mechanism

- [ ] Identify primary vs. fallback strategies

 {"id":"bd-njj0","title":"Update documentation for rich output features","description":"## Task Overview
Update all documentation to reflect the new rich output features, including README, AGENTS.md, and inline code documentation.

## Background & Reasoning

### Documentation Updates Needed
1. README.md - User-facing documentation
2. AGENTS.md - AI agent guidelines
3. Code comments - Developer documentation
4. Examples - Updated usage examples

### Why Documentation Matters
- Users need to know about new features
- Agents need to know robot mode is safe
- Contributors need to understand architecture
- Screenshots show visual improvements

## Implementation Details

### README.md Updates

#### New Features Section
```markdown
## Rich Terminal Output

caut provides beautiful, styled terminal output for human users while
maintaining clean, parseable output for AI agents.

### Human Mode (Default)
When running in a terminal, caut displays:
- Styled provider cards with color-coded branding
- Professional tables with formatted numbers
- Progress indicators during fetches
- Helpful error messages with suggestions

\![Usage output example](docs/screenshots/usage-output.png)

### Robot Mode (-r, --robot)
For AI agents and scripts, use robot mode:
```bash
caut usage --robot  # Clean JSON output
```

Robot mode guarantees:
- No ANSI escape codes
- Consistent JSON/Markdown format
- Machine-parseable output
- Full compatibility with all automation tools

### Theming
Customize appearance with themes:
```bash
export CAUT_THEME=minimal     # Subtle, less colorful
export CAUT_THEME=high-contrast  # Maximum readability
export CAUT_THEME=ascii       # ASCII-only characters
```

### Disabling Rich Output
```bash
# Standard NO_COLOR support
export NO_COLOR=1

# caut-specific
export CAUT_PLAIN=1
```
```

#### Updated Screenshots
- [ ] Usage command rich output
- [ ] Cost command visualization
- [ ] Doctor command diagnostics
- [ ] History command timeline
- [ ] Error panel example
- [ ] Help output example

### AGENTS.md Updates

```markdown
## Output Modes

### For AI Agents: Use Robot Mode
When using caut programmatically, ALWAYS use robot mode:

```bash
caut usage --robot
caut cost --robot --period 30d
```

Robot mode guarantees:
1. **No ANSI codes** - Output is plain text
2. **JSON format** - Parseable with standard JSON tools
3. **Stable schema** - Output format is versioned
4. **No visual elements** - No spinners, progress bars, or decorations

### DO NOT Parse Human Mode Output
Human mode output includes ANSI escape codes for styling and may change
format between versions. Always use --robot for automation.

### Environment Variables for Agents
These ensure plain output even if --robot is forgotten:
```bash
export CAUT_PLAIN=1  # Force plain output
export NO_COLOR=1    # Standard no-color hint
```
```

### Code Documentation

#### Module-Level Documentation
```rust
//\! # Rich Output Module
//\!
//\! This module provides styled terminal output for human users while
//\! maintaining compatibility with AI agent consumers.
//\!
//\! ## Safety Guarantees
//\!
//\! All rich output is gated behind safety checks. Robot mode (-r) and
//\! non-TTY output NEVER receive ANSI escape codes.
//\!
//\! ## Components
//\!
//\! - [] - Styled provider usage display
//\! - [] - Formatted usage summary table
//\! - [] - Helpful error messages
//\! - [] - Customizable styling
//\!
//\! ## Example
//\!
//\! ```rust
//\! use caut::rich::prelude::*;
//\!
//\! let args = Args::parse();
//\! if should_use_rich_output(&args) {
//\!     let console = Console::new();
//\!     let theme = get_theme(&args);
//\!     let card = ProviderCard::new(&usage, &theme);
//\!     console.print_renderable(&card.render());
//\! }
//\! ```
```

 {"id":"bd-o79.1","title":"Unit tests: File watcher and daemon core","description":"Comprehensive unit test suite for file watcher and daemon process management.

## Background
The daemon is a critical component that must be highly reliable. Thorough testing is essential since it runs continuously in the background.

## Test Categories

### 1. File Watcher Tests (src/daemon/watcher_tests.rs)
- Watch path configuration for all three providers
- Event classification (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini)
- Debouncing behavior (multiple rapid changes)
- Missing directory handling
- New directory creation detection
- File deletion handling
- Symlink handling

### 2. Credential Hashing Tests
- SHA-256 hash computation correctness
- Change detection (same content = no change)
- Change detection (different content = change)
- Binary file handling
- Large file handling
- Empty file handling

### 3. Change Detection Tests
- Detect account switch (different email)
- Detect token refresh (same email, different token)
- Detect first-time credential creation
- State persistence across restarts

### 4. Daemon Process Tests
- PID file creation and cleanup
- Duplicate instance prevention
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP)
- Graceful shutdown sequence
- Crash recovery behavior

### 5. Event Processing Tests
- Event queue handling
- Event ordering guarantees
- Error isolation (one provider failure doesn't affect others)

## Test Utilities
- Mock filesystem using tempdir
- Mock signal delivery
- Time control for debouncing tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File watcher tests (8+ test cases)
- [ ] Credential hashing tests (6+ test cases)
- [ ] Change detection tests (4+ test cases)
- [ ] Daemon process tests (5+ test cases)
- [ ] Event processing tests (3+ test cases)
- [ ] All tests pass in CI
- [ ] Code coverage >= 80% for daemon module","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-01-27T15:26:56.179801-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T15:26:56.179801-05:00","labels":["daemon","testing","unit-tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o79.1","depends_on_id":"bd-o79","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T15:26:56.184661-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"bd-o79.1","depends_on_id":"bd-biu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T15:31:38.658519-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"bd-o79.1","depends_on_id":"bd-uf0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T15:31:38.724279-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

## Test Architecture

### Test Environment
```rust
struct TestEnvironment {
    temp_dir: TempDir,
    daemon_handle: Option<DaemonHandle>,
    mock_providers: MockProviders,
    database: UsageDatabase,
}

impl TestEnvironment {
    fn new() -> Self { ... }
    fn create_mock_credential(&self, provider: Provider, content: &str) { ... }
    fn modify_credential(&self, provider: Provider, content: &str) { ... }
    fn wait_for_snapshot(&self, timeout: Duration) -> Option<UsageSnapshot> { ... }
}
```

## Test Scenarios

### Daemon Lifecycle (5 tests)
- [ ] test_daemon_starts_successfully - Daemon starts, writes PID file
- [ ] test_daemon_prevents_duplicate - Cannot start second instance
- [ ] test_daemon_stops_gracefully - SIGTERM triggers clean shutdown
- [ ] test_daemon_socket_listening - Unix socket accepts connections
- [ ] test_daemon_status_reporting - Status command returns correct info

### Credential Detection (8 tests)
- [ ] test_detect_claude_credential_create - New Claude cred triggers snapshot
- [ ] test_detect_claude_credential_change - Claude account switch detected
- [ ] test_detect_codex_credential_create - New Codex cred triggers snapshot
- [ ] test_detect_codex_credential_change - Codex account switch detected
- [ ] test_detect_gemini_credential_create - New Gemini cred triggers snapshot
- [ ] test_detect_gemini_credential_change - Gemini account switch detected
- [ ] test_debounce_rapid_changes - Multiple rapid changes debounced
- [ ] test_ignore_metadata_only_changes - Touch without content change ignored

### Snapshot Capture (6 tests)
- [ ] test_snapshot_on_credential_change - Snapshot stored on account switch
- [ ] test_snapshot_data_accuracy - Snapshot contains correct usage data
- [ ] test_periodic_snapshot_capture - Periodic snapshots at interval
- [ ] test_manual_snapshot_via_cli - Manual snapshot via CLI command
- [ ] test_snapshot_with_mock_api_failure - Graceful degradation on API error
- [ ] test_account_auto_registration - New accounts added to registry

### Multi-Account Queries (5 tests)
- [ ] test_list_all_accounts - List returns all registered accounts
- [ ] test_query_single_account_usage - Query specific account history
- [ ] test_aggregate_provider_usage - Aggregate across provider accounts
- [ ] test_all_accounts_flag - --all-accounts aggregates correctly
- [ ] test_account_labeling - Labels stored and queryable

### Error Recovery (4 tests)
- [ ] test_recover_from_api_failure - Daemon continues after API error
- [ ] test_recover_from_db_error - Daemon recovers from DB write failure
- [ ] test_handle_missing_cred_dir - Daemon starts without all provider dirs
- [ ] test_handle_permission_error - Graceful handling of permission denied

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 28 test scenarios pass
- [ ] Tests run in CI (GitHub Actions)
- [ ] Tests use temporary directories (no system pollution)
- [ ] Tests clean up all resources on completion
- [ ] Tests dont require real credentials (use mocks)
- [ ] Tests complete in under 60 seconds total
